title: How Little John Joined Robin Hood
titleZh: 小约翰如何加入罗宾汉

q.en: Why did Robin Hood steal from the rich?

options.en: To return the money to the poor

explain.en: Robin vowed to bring justice by stealing from the rich and giving the money back to the poor.
explain.zh: 罗宾发誓劫富济贫，以此伸张正义。

q.en: What was John Little's loyalty before meeting Robin?
q.zh: 遇到罗宾之前，小约翰效忠于谁？

options.en: No one, he was loyal to no one
options.zh: 谁也不效忠，他谁也不依附

explain.en: The book says John Little was loyal to no one, neither lords nor outlaws, and felt safest by himself.
explain.zh: 书中说小约翰谁也不效忠，既不属领主也不属绿林，独来独往最自在。

q.en: Why did Robin fight John Little with oak branches instead of his bow?
q.zh: 罗宾为什么用橡树枝而不是弓箭和小约翰打斗？

options.en: It would be unfair since the stranger had no bow
options.zh: 因为对方没有弓，用弓不公平

options.en: The bridge was too narrow for a bow

explain.en: Robin cut oak branches so the fight would be fair, since the stranger had no bow or arrows.
explain.zh: 罗宾砍下橡树枝，因为对方没有弓箭，这样较量才公平。

q.en: Who won the fight on the bridge?

options.en: John Little, who knocked Robin into the water
options.zh: 小约翰，他把罗宾打进了水里

explain.en: John gave Robin a blow that made him stagger, then smacked him into the water; Robin laughed and admitted he was beaten.
explain.zh: 小约翰一击让罗宾踉跄，随后把他打落水中；罗宾大笑认输。

q.en: What did Robin Hood do after losing the fight?
q.zh: 罗宾输掉打斗后做了什么？

options.en: Ordered his men to attack John
Laughed and invited John to join his band
options.zh: 大笑并邀请小约翰加入队伍

explain.en: Robin said it was a fair fight and asked the stranger to join, because he wanted brave men in his band.
explain.zh: 罗宾说这是公平较量，并邀请对方入伙，因为他需要勇敢的人。

q.en: What condition did John Little set for joining the band?
q.zh: 小约翰加入队伍提出了什么条件？

options.en: That he be paid in gold
That someone shoot a bow better than he could

explain.en: John said he would join only if one of them could shoot a bow more accurately than he could.
explain.zh: 小约翰说，除非有人射箭比他更准，否则他才肯加入。

q.en: How did Robin Hood win the archery match?
q.zh: 罗宾汉怎样赢得了射箭比赛？

options.en: He shot closer to the center
He split John's arrow with his own shot
options.zh: 他一箭劈开了小约翰的箭

options.en: He let John miss on purpose

explain.en: Robin's arrow struck John's arrow dead-on and splintered it, proving he was the better shot.
explain.zh: 罗宾的箭正中并劈碎了小约翰的箭，证明他射术更胜一筹。

q.en: Why did the Merry Men laugh when John said his name?
q.zh: 小约翰说出自己名字时，快乐汉们为什么大笑？

options.en: 'Little' did not fit his huge size
options.zh: “小”这个名和他在高大身材根本不搭

explain.en: Will Scarlet said 'Little' did not describe his puny stature, so they renamed him Little John.
explain.zh: 威尔·斯卡利特说“小”配不上他矮小的身材，于是给他改名“小约翰”。

q.en: What is the main focus of this folktale?
q.zh: 这个民间故事主要讲什么？

options.en: The friendship and loyalty between Robin and his bravest follower
options.zh: 罗宾与他最勇敢的追随者之间的友谊与忠诚

explain.en: The story shows how Little John earned Robin's trust and became his loyal second-in-command.
explain.zh: 故事讲述小约翰如何赢得罗宾的信任，成为他忠诚的副手。

q.en: What role did Little John take after joining?
q.zh: 小约翰加入后担任什么角色？

explain.en: From that day on Little John was Robin's second-in-command, the best among them next to Robin.
explain.zh: 从那天起，小约翰成为罗宾的副手，仅次于罗宾的最强者。

prompt.en: John Little only joined after proving himself in a fair fight. Do you think earning respect through a fair test is a good way to build trust? Why or why not?
prompt.zh: 小约翰是在公平比试证明自己后才加入的。你认为通过公平考验来赢得信任是好办法吗？为什么？

en: Consider what makes someone trustworthy in your own experience.
Think about whether a test of skill is fairer than simply being told to obey.
zh: 想想你自己的经验中，什么让人值得信任。
思考能力考验是否比单纯服从更公平。
